introSection:
Cheetah was a wooden roller coaster by Custom Coasters International, located in the African Pridelands Section of the Wild Adventures theme park in Valdosta, Georgia, USA. The coaster was named after the Cheetah, the fastest land animal, because of its top speed reaching when the coaster first opened, but after years of friction and track deformation, the coaster was recorded in 2009 reaching a top speed of.

Built in 2001, Cheetah was the largest roller coaster at Wild Adventures.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Cheetah opened on June 16, 2001 following an investment of $3.5 million.

After the 2009 season, Cheetah was given a $1.3 million renovation by Martin & Vleminckx replacing the track on the figure-eight and smoothing out the rest of the layout.

In early 2021, Wild Adventures announced that Cheetah would never reopen. It was demolished completely later in the year. The station building was retained and repurposed as a picnic shelter, with the ride's train remaining in place as décor. The coaster was replaced by the Safari Campground.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Cheetah had a standard out and back layout with five bunny hops and a figure-eight ending.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: Cheetah began with a tall lift hill and drop to a small  bunny hop, followed by a larger  bunny hop. It continued with a 35-degree incline. Cheetah's return journey started with a drop and two more  bunny hops, ending with a figure eight.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were made by Gerstlauer.

fullText:
Cheetah was a wooden roller coaster by Custom Coasters International, located in the African Pridelands Section of the Wild Adventures theme park in Valdosta, Georgia, USA. The coaster was named after the Cheetah, the fastest land animal, because of its top speed reaching when the coaster first opened, but after years of friction and track deformation, the coaster was recorded in 2009 reaching a top speed of.

Built in 2001, Cheetah was the largest roller coaster at Wild Adventures.

Cheetah opened on June 16, 2001 following an investment of $3.5 million.

After the 2009 season, Cheetah was given a $1.3 million renovation by Martin & Vleminckx replacing the track on the figure-eight and smoothing out the rest of the layout.

In early 2021, Wild Adventures announced that Cheetah would never reopen. It was demolished completely later in the year. The station building was retained and repurposed as a picnic shelter, with the ride's train remaining in place as décor. The coaster was replaced by the Safari Campground.

Cheetah had a standard out and back layout with five bunny hops and a figure-eight ending.

Cheetah began with a tall lift hill and drop to a small  bunny hop, followed by a larger  bunny hop. It continued with a 35-degree incline. Cheetah's return journey started with a drop and two more  bunny hops, ending with a figure eight.

The trains were made by Gerstlauer.
